# MAF Migration Report — mixed

Files scanned: 2  |  AutoGen generation: mixed

## SUMMARY

  Total unique constructs: 4

  ✓ AUTO          2  constructs  (low — mechanical rename)
  ~ PARTIAL       0  constructs  (medium — config/param changes needed)
  ✗ MANUAL        1  construct  (high — pattern rewrite required)
  ? UNKNOWN       1  construct  (unknown — not in mapping database)

## CONSTRUCTS

### ✓ AUTO — low effort (mechanical rename)

  AssistantAgent                            
    from autogen_agentchat.agents
    →  Agent (agent_framework)
    Note: model_client= → client=; system_message= → instructions=;
          max_tool_iterations= dropped (MAF handles loop termination
          differently). FunctionTool wrappers become @tool-decorated functions
          passed directly.

  TextMessage                               
    from autogen_agentchat.messages
    →  Message (agent_framework)
    Note: TextMessage(content=..., source=...) → Message(role=...,
          contents=[...]). source= string maps to role=; content string becomes a
          single-item list.

### ✗ MANUAL — high effort (pattern rewrite required)

  ConversableAgent                          
    from autogen
    →  (no direct MAF equivalent)
    Note: Base class for 0.2 agents. No direct MAF mapping — subclasses typically
          map to Agent with instructions= and tools=; override logic must be
          refactored manually.

### ? UNKNOWN — not in mapping database

  AssistantAgent                            
    from pyautogen
    →  (no direct MAF equivalent)

## DESTINATION NOTE

This report targets Microsoft Agent Framework (MAF) as the migration destination.

AG2 alternative: some constructs in this project are marked AG2-compatible —
AG2 preserves more of the original AutoGen API surface and may be a lower-effort
target if your team is not committed to MAF. See construct notes above (ag2_note).

Run `maf-migrate run <path>` to apply AUTO transforms automatically (pro tier).
